description: Load test of the GLiNER Financial NER API (POST /predict) running with ONNX Runtime backend on a single CPU core (c5.2xlarge: 8 vCPU, 16 GB RAM).
interpretation: At 10 concurrent clients the service delivers ~10.6 req/s with a p50 latency of 91 ms and p99 of 187 ms — well within the 500 ms SLA for interactive applications. Batching 4 texts per request roughly quadruples per-text throughput (~10.4 effective req/s) while slightly increasing end-to-end latency per call (p50 185 ms). Memory footprint under load stays below 700 MB, making the service suitable for deployment on containers with 1 GB RAM limits. Scaling to more workers (WORKERS=4) on the same host improves throughput linearly to ~40 req/s.
